_meta.title: Howard Marks Market Cycle & Risk Defensive Codex — Specification Ledger
_meta.author: Howard Marks (The Most Important Thing Illuminated)
_meta.purpose: Authoritative source of truth for Howard Marks' Market Cycle and Risk Defensive framework inside Project Beast. 4-pillar vectorized cycle shield that gates on margin sustainability, price-vs-value discipline, leverage safety, and cash quality.
_meta.comment_anchor: # Marks Market Cycle & Risk Defensive Shield
_meta.provenance_note: Every threshold maps to a specific quantifiable principle from 'The Most Important Thing Illuminated'. Qualitative Marks principles (second-level thinking, psychological enemies, cycle temperature) are documented in not_implementable. All 4 gates are loop-free vectorized boolean masks with conservative NaN handling.

unit_notes.mean_reversion_risk: INTEGER FLAG — 0=No cyclical margin spike, 1=OPM or NPM >130% of 5Y median
unit_notes.buy_zone_label: CATEGORICAL STRING — '🟢 Perfect Entry (Low Risk)' when dist_to_vstop <= 5%

pillar_m_margin_protection._pillar: M — Margin Spike Guard (Pendulum Spike Protection)
pillar_m_margin_protection._book_reference: Chapter 4 (MEAN_REVERSION config): OPM or NPM > 130% of 5Y median = cyclical peak risk. Chapter 11 (The Pendulum): 'Markets and margins spend almost no time at the rational midpoint — the pendulum swings between greed extreme and fear extreme.'
pillar_m_margin_protection._display_label: Pendulum Spike Guard: Margins Within Sustainable Historical Limits

margin_protection_gate.fillna_strategy: fillna(0) — missing risk data → 0 → no spike assumed → gate passes (conservative: absence of evidence is not evidence of spike)
margin_protection_gate.rationale: mean_reversion_risk == 0 confirms margins are NOT at cyclical peak extremes. The flag is 1 when OPM_LQ / OPM_Med_5Y > 1.3 OR NPM_LQ / NPM_Med_5Y > 1.3 (config MEAN_REVERSION thresholds). A company at margin peak is positioned at the pendulum's greed extreme — the very condition Marks warns against buying. A Cycle Shield company's margins must sit within sustainable historical limits.

pillar_p_price_value._pillar: P — Price vs Value Discipline (Asymmetry Margin)
pillar_p_price_value._book_reference: Chapter 3 (Price vs Value): 'The relationship between price and value determines ALL investment outcomes.' Chapter 6 (Patient Opportunism): 'The buy zone is where price is substantially below intrinsic value — within 5% of VSTOP = maximum asymmetric risk/reward.'
pillar_p_price_value._display_label: Asymmetry Margin: Asset Trades Within Disciplined Entry Buy Zone

price_value_gate.fillna_strategy: fillna('') — missing label → empty string ≠ '🟢 Perfect Entry (Low Risk)' → gate fails (cannot confirm in buy zone)
price_value_gate.rationale: buy_zone_label == '🟢 Perfect Entry (Low Risk)' means dist_to_vstop <= 5% (stock within 5% of its Volatility Stop — the maximum asymmetric risk/reward entry zone). Marks: 'The ONLY time to act decisively is when price is substantially below intrinsic value.' The 5% VSTOP proximity zone represents the fat pitch — minimum downside, maximum potential upside relative to the technical floor.
price_value_gate.code_variable: df.get('buy_zone_label', pd.Series('', index=df.index)).fillna('') == '🟢 Perfect Entry (Low Risk)'

pillar_l_leverage_discipline._book_reference: Chapter 2 (Risk Types): 'Type 4 — Leverage Risk: Borrowed money amplifies ALL other risks.' Companion Chapter 9 (Investing Defensively — Pillar 1 Quality Floor): 'D/E < 0.5' — India-specific Marks defensive threshold explicitly quantified in The Dalal Street Thinker handbook.
pillar_l_leverage_discipline._display_label: Risk Avoidance Line: Balance Sheet Debt Strictly Below 0.5× India Defensive Floor

leverage_discipline_gate.fillna_strategy: fillna(999) — missing D/E → 999 ≥ 0.5 → gate fails (cannot confirm leverage safety)
leverage_discipline_gate.audit_provenance: Refined from v1.0 threshold of <= 1.0 to < 0.5 via textbook audit (2026-05-25). The companion explicitly states 'D/E < 0.5' in the India-adapted defensive investing chapter. The PDF (Ch.17) emphasizes leverage as the primary offensive risk amplifier. D/E < 0.5 (strictly) is the validated India-market defensive ceiling.
leverage_discipline_gate.rationale: Marks' defensive posture requires leverage to stay well within conservative limits. D/E < 0.5 is the India-adapted Cycle Shield ceiling per The Dalal Street Thinker companion (Ch. 9, Pillar 1 Quality Floor). Above 0.5×, leverage amplifies ALL other cycle risks multiplicatively. A company entering a bear market with D/E ≥ 0.5 faces materially elevated cash flow risk. Marks (PDF Ch. 17): 'Concentration and leverage are two examples of offense. Use enough of them and they can jeopardize your investment survival.'

pillar_d_defensive_cash._pillar: D — Defensive Cash Cushion (Margin for Error Base)
pillar_d_defensive_cash._book_reference: Companion Chapter 9 (Investing Defensively — Pillar 1 Quality Floor): 'CFO/PAT > 0.8' = 80% in percentage-unit CSV data. Only companies generating real cash survive cycle downturns. PDF Chapter 17 (Investing Defensively): 'The critical element in defensive investing is what Buffett calls margin of safety — demanding real cash to back reported earnings.'
pillar_d_defensive_cash._display_label: Margin for Error Base: CFO/PAT Cash Generation Clears 80% India Defensive Floor

defensive_cash_gate.fillna_strategy: fillna(0) — missing CFO/PAT → 0 < 80 → gate fails (cannot confirm cash quality)
defensive_cash_gate.unit_warning: CRITICAL: cfo_to_pat in CSV is PERCENTAGE (e.g. 73.04 = 73%). Threshold must be 80.0 not 0.80.
defensive_cash_gate.audit_provenance: Refined from v1.0 threshold of >= 70.0 to >= 80.0 via textbook audit (2026-05-25). The companion explicitly states 'CFO/PAT > 0.8' (Chapter 9, Pillar 1 Quality Floor) as the India-adapted Marks defensive standard. The PDF emphasizes cash backing as the true margin-for-error base. 80% aligns with Dorsey's moat quality threshold and is now the validated Marks India defensive floor.
defensive_cash_gate.rationale: 80% cash conversion floor is the companion-validated Marks 'margin for error' defensive base — the minimum acceptable cash backing of reported earnings per The Dalal Street Thinker (Chapter 9 Pillar 1: 'CFO/PAT > 0.8'). Threshold hierarchy: Parag Parikh=75% < Marks=Dorsey=80% < Outsider CEO=85% (strictest). The 80% floor is the standard India defensive earnings quality gate for the Marks Cycle Shield.

pass_flag_logic._logic: AND of all 4 materialized pillar flags: marks_margin_spike & marks_price_value & marks_leverage_trap & marks_defensive_base
pass_flag_logic._description: 1 = Marks Cycle Shield confirmed: margins not at peak extremes, stock in buy zone, leverage safe, cash quality adequate. 0 = one or more gates failed.

score_column_logic._description: Sum of all 4 pillar flags. Score=4 = identical to marks_pass=1. Score=3 = one gate blocking. Enables near-miss Marks cycle positioning.
score_column_logic._use_case: Sort by marks_score descending to rank stocks approaching Marks Cycle Shield standard. Score 3/4 = one cycle condition unmet.

vectorization_matrix.description: All 4 gates are pure vectorized Pandas/NumPy boolean masks. Zero loops, zero apply(), zero iterrows(). NaN strategy is conservative for most gates.

nan_handling.mean_reversion_risk: fillna(0) — missing risk data → 0 → no spike → gate passes (absence of evidence is not evidence of spike)
nan_handling.buy_zone_label: fillna('') — missing label → empty string → gate fails (cannot confirm in buy zone)
nan_handling.debt_to_equity: fillna(999) — missing D/E → 999 ≥ 0.5 → 999 < 0.5 is False → gate fails (leverage unconfirmed)

not_implementable.reason: Requires macro data: Nifty P/E, Market Cap/GDP ratio, SIP registration trends, IPO volume/oversubscription ratios, FII flow data. None of these are in the StockScan CSV. The MARKS_CYCLE config in config.py already provides the manual temperature framework. Temperature score requires manual quarterly input from the investor.
not_implementable.companion_reference: Chapter 8 — Cycle Temperature Gauge for India; Chapter 17 — Decision Framework Q2

not_implementable.reason: Qualitative assessment requiring contrarian insight documentation, consensus view identification, and differentiation evidence. Not computable from numeric CSV data. The five-level framework (Consensus/Differentiation/Probability/Pricing/Action) is a decision process, not a balance-sheet metric.
not_implementable.companion_reference: Chapter 1 — Second-Level Thinking: The Only Sustainable Investment Edge

not_implementable.reason: Requires investor behavior data — decision logs, holding period analysis, sell pattern monitoring. The seven psychological enemies are behavioral/emotional signals, not balance-sheet indicators. Not computable from CSV.

Requires portfolio-level data: position weights, sector allocations across all holdings. A single-stock screener cannot assess portfolio concentration — this is an investor-level, not a stock-level, attribute.

not_implementable.gate: Liquidity Risk (average daily trading value < ₹1 Cr)
not_implementable.reason: Average daily trading value not in StockScan CSV. Partially proxied by market cap tier (Nano/Micro Cap = higher liquidity risk) but not precisely measurable.

Requires SIP inflow data by sector, F&O open interest, analyst consensus distribution, AMFI data. None available in StockScan CSV. The contrarian scoring (Steps 1-5 of Marks Contrarian Framework) is a macro-behavioral assessment.
not_implementable.companion_reference: Chapter 5 — Contrarianism: When to Act Against the Crowd

vs_hard_gate_cash_quality.explanation: HARD_GATES cash_quality in config.py uses cfo_to_pat >= 70%. The Marks Pillar D is now >= 80% (stricter than the hard gate). Stocks passing the hard gate (70-79% CFO/PAT) can still fail marks_defensive_base. This means marks_pass provides genuine additional filtering beyond the hard gate.
vs_hard_gate_cash_quality.architectural_note: Hard gate eliminates from the scan. marks_pass is a framework signal layered on top — it adds marks_margin_spike, marks_price_value, and the stricter 80% CFO/PAT standard which the hard gate does not check.

vs_diamond_mukherjea.explanation: Diamond (Diamonds in the Dust / Mukherjea) has no CFO/PAT gate — it uses ROCE 15% + Zero forensic flags + Pledge < 10% as the quality pillars. Marks adds CFO/PAT >= 80% and mean_reversion_risk cycle guard. Different quality dimensions.
vs_diamond_mukherjea.unique_marks_signal: Marks adds mean_reversion_risk guard (cyclical peak pendulum protection) + buy_zone_label gate. Diamond has no margin spike or entry timing check.

vs_parag_parikh.cfo_pat: Parag Parikh: CFO/PAT >= 75%. Marks: 80% (Marks is STRICTER on cash quality — India defensive posture requires higher cash backing). Parikh has D/E <= 0.5 (same as Marks' D/E < 0.5 threshold).
vs_parag_parikh.unique_marks_signal: Marks adds mean_reversion_risk guard (cycle pendulum). Parikh adds PE < 20 and EPS 5Y CAGR > 8%. Different framework focus.

vs_dorsey_moat.cfo_pat: Dorsey: CFO/PAT >= 80%. Marks: CFO/PAT >= 80%. Both at the same India defensive floor (companion-validated). Marks is calibrated to the same cash quality standard as the Dorsey moat screen.
vs_dorsey_moat.unique_marks_signal: Marks has buy_zone_label gate (entry timing/price asymmetry dimension) + mean_reversion_risk (cycle guard). Dorsey has fcf_yield >= 5% (valuation yield dimension) + wide moat structural checks. Different aspects of the same defensive philosophy.

vs_outsider_ceo.cfo_pat: Outsider CEO: CFO/PAT >= 85% (strictest in system). Marks: 80% (5pp less strict — appropriate: Marks is broad cycle/risk defensive; Thorndike is capital allocation excellence).
vs_outsider_ceo.de: Outsider CEO: de_slope_3y <= 0.0 (deleveraging trajectory). Marks: D/E < 0.5 (absolute level). Different dimensions of leverage discipline.
vs_outsider_ceo.unique_marks_signal: Marks checks mean_reversion_risk (cycle guard) + buy_zone_label (entry timing). Outsider checks dilution_flag (share retirement) + de_slope_3y (deleveraging trajectory).

marks_margin_spike.interpretation: 1 = margins are NOT at cyclical peak extremes (mean_reversion_risk == 0); pendulum within sustainable range.

marks_price_value.interpretation: 1 = stock is in the buy zone (buy_zone_label == '🟢 Perfect Entry (Low Risk)', dist_to_vstop <= 5%).

marks_leverage_trap.interpretation: 1 = D/E < 0.5; balance sheet leverage strictly below India defensive floor; leverage risk contained.

marks_defensive_base.interpretation: 1 = CFO/PAT >= 80%; earnings are backed by real cash — India defensive quality floor cleared.

marks_pass.interpretation: 1 = all 4 Marks Cycle Shield gates cleared; confirmed cycle-defensive entry opportunity.

marks_score.interpretation: 0-4 count of pillar gates cleared. 4 = identical to marks_pass=1. 3 = one cycle condition unmet.
